logistics.explanation: Race starts at Camilo Cano Olympic Stadium in La Nucia with jersey pickup the day before from 4-9 PM. Online registration only via granfondolanucia.com with no on-site options. UCI GFWS organization ensures smooth qualifiers for 2027 Championships.

length.explanation: 132 km route challenges riders over 82 miles through Costa Blanca mountains. Designed by Miguel Indurain for a full-day effort from La Nucia to interior Alicante. Qualifies for UCI Gran Fondo World Championships post-2026 event.

technicality.explanation: 100% paved roads with high-quality asphalt on climbs like Alto de Aitana. No gravel or singletrack, focusing on endurance over bike handling. Steady gradients like 5.5% early 3.6 km ascent suit road setups.

elevation.explanation: 2275 m gain over 132 km features Alto de Aitana summit above 1000 m after 45 km. Initial climbs to Finestrat and Relleu set demanding tone from La Nucia. First feedzone at Aitana high point aids sustained efforts.

climate.explanation: October in Costa Blanca offers mild Mediterranean weather ideal for late-season riding. La Nucia's coastal location near Benidorm beaches ensures comfortable temps. Post-World Champs timing avoids peak summer heat.

altitude.explanation: Highest point at Alto de Aitana over 1000 m provides moderate hypoxia challenge. Route stays mostly below 1000 m through Alicante mountains from La Nucia. Suitable for sea-level trained riders.

adventure.explanation: Mountainous interior from La Nucia via Finestrat, Relleu to Alto de Aitana delivers epic climbs. Spectacular roads and historic passes in Costa Blanca. Pre-race criterium with stars like Pedro Delgado adds thrill.

prestige.explanation: UCI Gran Fondo World Series qualifier for 2027 Championships in France. Course by Miguel Indurain, 5x Tour de France winner. Joins elite Spanish events on international calendar.

race_quality.explanation: UCI-endorsed with 132 km, 2275 m route through Alicante's legendary climbs. Organizational excellence in La Nucia, City of Sport. Features International Criterium with pros like Isaac del Toro.

experience.explanation: 100% cycling atmosphere from La Nucia start with iconic ascents and views. Pre-event criterium showcases legends like Samuel Sánchez. Complete challenge blending competition and Costa Blanca scenery.

community.explanation: Draws 2000 cyclists to La Nucia's Olympic Stadium for shared qualifiers. Winter training hub for WorldTour teams fosters camaraderie. Global UCI series attracts international amateurs.

field_depth.explanation: Capacity around 2000 participants for UCI qualifier in La Nucia. Mix of Spanish and international riders targeting 2027 Worlds spots. Growing field since 2024 founding.

value.explanation: UCI GFWS prestige with Indurain-designed 132 km route for qualifier entry. Access to criterium spectacle in La Nucia. High-quality organization at regional costs.

expenses.explanation: Affordable access from Alicante Airport, 30 min to La Nucia. Nearby Benidorm and Guadalest options keep lodging reasonable. No refunds but online-only registration simplifies budgeting.

cultural_impact.explanation: La Nucia as City of Sport hosts national draw with UCI global reach. Costa Blanca training hub elevates Valencian cycling scene. Events like Delgado criterium build local legacy.

course_description.summary: The 132 km Granfondo La Nucia route, designed by Miguel Indurain, winds from La Nucia through Costa Blanca mountains with 2275 m elevation gain. Riders tackle early climbs to Finestrat and Relleu, culminating at Alto de Aitana summit above 1000 m. Iconic paved ascents and spectacular roads deliver a demanding qualifier for UCI Gran Fondo World Series.

key_segments.name: Early Climb
key_segments.description: First ascent 5 km from La Nucia start, 3.6 km at 5.5% gradient.

key_segments.name: Alto de Aitana
key_segments.description: Major climb from km 20 to 45, race's highest point over 1000 m with first feedzone.

key_segments.name: Finestrat-Relleu
key_segments.description: Initial mountain rise into Alicante interior post-La Nucia.

course_description.elevation_profile: Challenging profile with steady early ramps building to prolonged Alto de Aitana climb peaking at 1000+ m; total 2275 m gain over 132 km.
course_description.aid_stations: First at Alto de Aitana summit after 45 km.

travel.accommodation: La Nucia hotels; nearby Benidorm beaches 20 min
travel.bike_transport: Fly to ALC; stadium bike assembly area

race_day.start_time: 8:00 AM at Camilo Cano Olympic Stadium
race_day.awards: UCI GFWS qualifiers; age group podiums

training_recommendations.minimum_fitness: Base of 3000+ km/season with hill reps; FTP 3.5+ w/kg for climbs.
training_recommendations.ideal_preparation: 8-12 weeks building to 132 km rides with 2000-2500 m elevation; focus Aitana-like 20+ km efforts.
training_recommendations.key_workouts: 3x 20-min threshold at 5-6%; 4-5 hr endurance with 1500 m hills; back-to-back long days.

history.notable_facts: First UCI GFWS edition in 2026 as 2027 qualifier; course by Miguel Indurain; criterium stars include Pedro Delgado, Isaac del Toro.

final_verdict.one_liner: Indurain-designed Costa Blanca beast: UCI qualifier with Aitana summit suffering.
final_verdict.who_should_ride: Strong amateurs chasing Worlds spots; hill climbers loving paved epics.
final_verdict.who_should_skip: Gravel purists or low-elevation riders under 4000 km/year.
final_verdict.insider_tip: Arrive early for criterium vibe Saturday; fuel heavy before Aitana feedzone at 45 km.

suffering_zones.label: Alto de Aitana Grind
suffering_zones.description: 25 km effort from km 20 peaking above 1000 m; relentless gradients shred legs mid-race.

suffering_zones.label: Finestrat-Relleu Ramp-Up
suffering_zones.description: Early Alicante interior climbs post-5 km starter; heat legs before big Aitana.

suffering_zones.label: Late Mountain Descent-Remontée
suffering_zones.description: Post-Aitana rollers demand recovery power over final 80+ km to La Nucia.
